title: unsafe impl Send for Device relies on unverifiable C library claim
description: device.rs line 20-21: 'SAFETY: WOLFTPM2_DEV holds no thread-local state and no raw pointers shared across threads.' This claim is not verifiable from this codebase — it requires reading wolfTPM internals. If wolfTPM adds global or thread-unsafe state in a future version, the Send impl becomes unsound and there is no compile-time detector. Minimum: document exactly which wolfTPM internal contract is being relied upon (e.g. no global mutable state outside DEV, Init is not called concurrently). Ideally add a note to the upgrade checklist.
